programs.details: Parents of minor children can be paid providers if they meet specific criteria, including the lack of alternative care options and the child requiring 24-hour supervision.

programs.agency: Texas Health and Human Services Commission (HHSC)
programs.details: Available under MDCP, CLASS, and HCS waivers. Parents of minor children can be paid providers for certain personal care services through the CDS option.

programs.agency: Florida Agency for Persons with Disabilities (APD)
programs.details: Under the iBudget waiver, the CDC+ option allows legally responsible parents of minor children to be hired as paid caregivers and respite providers.

programs.program_name: Participant Directed Services / Services My Way
programs.details: Available under the Consolidated and Community Living waivers. Parents can hire themselves to provide respite and personal care if approved in the service plan.

programs.agency: New York State Department of Health (DOH)
programs.details: Parents of minor children (under 18) are prohibited from being their personal assistants. However, parents of adult children (18+) can be hired as CDPAP assistants.
Available under the Individual Options (IO) and Level One waivers. Parents of minor children can be paid providers for personal care services up to 40 hours per week.
Parents of adult children can be paid caregivers. Parents of minor children are generally excluded unless extraordinary care requirements are met under specific waiver guidelines.
Waivers allow family members to be hired for personal support services, but parents of minor children face strict eligibility reviews and must route through a licensed agency.

programs.agency: North Carolina Department of Health and Human Services (DHHS)
programs.details: Parents of minor children can be paid as personal care assistants under CAP/C if they meet the strict criteria for 'extraordinary care' and pass clinical assessments.

programs.agency: Michigan Department of Health and Human Services (MDHHS)
programs.details: Allows self-direction and family hiring of personal care assistants. Parents of minor children can be hired under specific CMHSP local board approvals.
